title: premna bengalensis
intro: Premna bengalensis, also known as Premna tomentosa, is a plant species of the family Lamiaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities. The health benefits of Premna bengalensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of Premna bengalensis include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in traditional cooking as a spice.
